description: ScoutLane resume + cover letter + assessment theme palette system. All themes share one ATS-safe, single-column skeleton; only `primary` and `accent` change between themes. `wash` is an optional light header tint (text on it MUST stay dark). Legibility never depends on a background fill.

rules.accentTextNote: Use `accent` for graphic elements (rules, markers, swatches, gauge arcs). Use `accentText` for any accent-colored TEXT (dates, tagline) — it is the accent darkened to >=4.5:1 on white for WCAG AA text. Picker UI must render theme accents only as graphics, never as text.

themes.name: Navy & Copper
themes.designer: Deep navy authority with a warm copper accent, a pairing most templates never reach for. Serif name on a faint blue-grey wash, copper rules and markers.
themes.recruiter: Premium and a little warm without being loud. Distinctive in a cold-blue insurance or finance stack; safe for conservative reviewers, still memorable.

themes.name: Ink & Teal
themes.designer: Deep petrol-ink with a clean teal. Cooler and more modern than copper; teal signals technical without going neon.
themes.recruiter: Current and engineering-forward. Great for cloud, platform, and DevOps roles at tech-leaning employers. More builder than executive.

themes.name: Charcoal & Amber
themes.designer: Near-black charcoal with a confident amber-gold. The neutral primary lets the warm amber do all the talking. Warm paper-tone wash.
themes.recruiter: Reads like a well-made editorial document. Amber is eye-catching among blue resumes. Versatile across industries; senior and design-aware.

themes.name: Forest & Stone
themes.designer: Deep forest green with a muted bronze-stone accent. Organic and grounded; green is rare on resumes so it registers instantly.
themes.recruiter: Calm, mature, uncommon. Stands out by being unexpected, not bright. Suits healthcare, manufacturing, operations leadership.

themes.name: Oxford & Burgundy
themes.designer: Oxford-blue with a deep burgundy accent, the most classically establishment pairing. Burgundy adds gravitas where copper adds warmth.
themes.recruiter: Seasoned, credible, executive. Excellent for finance, insurance, legal-adjacent, or director-track roles. Never looks risky.

themes.name: Slate & Rust
themes.designer: Cool blue-slate primary with a punchy rust-orange. Softer than navy, the rust gives it energy. Clean, contemporary, industrial.
themes.recruiter: Approachable but sharp. Less formal than navy, more vivid than teal. Good for mid-market and roles where modern-and-capable beats buttoned-up.

themes.name: Graphite & Electric Blue
themes.designer: Graphite with a saturated electric blue, the most overtly tech option. Bright blue used only on thin rules, markers, and dates so it never overwhelms.
themes.recruiter: Signals software, cloud, modern IT loudly. Best for startups and engineering orgs. Slightly bold for the most conservative insurers.

themes.name: Espresso & Sage
themes.designer: Warm espresso-brown primary with a soft sage-green accent. Unusual and warm; brown reads softer than black, sage is calming. Boutique feel.
themes.recruiter: Memorable for being warm and human among corporate blue. Suits design-aware, people-centric, or smaller-company cultures.

themes.name: Midnight & Gold
themes.designer: Very deep midnight-navy with an antique gold accent. The richest, most premium pairing; gold reads luxury when muted, not yellow.
themes.recruiter: Top-tier executive polish. Conveys seniority and prestige; great for leadership, principal, or director-level applications.

themes.name: Steel & Crimson
themes.designer: Steel-blue-grey primary with a clear crimson accent. The boldest warm accent in the set, high energy and high visibility, kept disciplined by clean structure.
themes.recruiter: Commands attention; crimson pulls the eye to the name and dates. Confident, a touch assertive. Strong for competitive roles where standing out fast matters.
